judgment.summary: Strong growth and margins are offset by high debt and negative free cash flow.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimate by 5.7%; next earnings date is 2026-06-10.
positives: Revenue growth 21.7% YoY and earnings growth 24.5% YoY indicate strong top-line and bottom-line expansion.
Profit margin 25.3% and Return on Equity 57.6% demonstrate high profitability and efficient use of shareholder equity.
Forward P/E of 23.64 is attractive relative to trailing P/E of 34.09, suggesting expected earnings growth.
Analyst consensus is a strong buy with a mean target price of $244.03, indicating significant upside potential.
negatives: Debt/Equity ratio of 415.26 is extremely high, indicating significant financial leverage.
Free cash flow is negative $-22.30B TTM, suggesting the company is burning cash.
Price/Sales ratio of 8.52 is high, indicating the stock may be expensive relative to its revenue.
Beta of 1.54 suggests higher volatility than the overall market.

news.paragraph: Oracle is expanding its AI integration in clinical research, a move that has garnered bullish sentiment from Wedbush who cited strong demand visibility. While a competitor's CEO is focused on surviving the AI era, Oracle appears to be actively leveraging the technology. The upcoming earnings report on June 10th will be a key catalyst to watch.

headlines.title: Workday’s Returning CEO Has a Plan to Survive the AI Era
Oracle Corporation (ORCL) Expands Clinical Research with AI Integration
Wedbush Remains Bullish On Oracle (ORCL), Says Investors Are Overlooking Demand Visibility

examples.body: $ORCL not sure why all these guys are freaking out on a red day. If anything I hope ORCL goes down to 180 so i can pickup more.
$ORCL has some decency than this turd $MSFT
$ORCL how much longer will institutions be accumulating sub $200?
$ORCL double handle cup and handle. Grinding up on $200. Break it and this thing flies north. This is at least 50% discounted by any conservative estimates
